_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis (o4 §1); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
This document covers the Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII) framework under MARPOL Annex VI, as adopted by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) and in force from 1 November 2022, with mandatory ship-level calculations commencing 1 January 2023. It applies to the workspace in its role as an ocean-mode freight forwarder operating across Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes, carrying cargo in the live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian verticals.

**Convention notes:** CII is an operational measure (per-voyage and annual), distinct from EEXI (a design/retrofit efficiency index). This document focuses on CII only. EEXI is noted where the two interact but is not analysed in full here. All regulatory citations are to MARPOL Annex VI as amended by IMO MEPC resolutions. Items requiring legal review are labelled **Legal Confirmation Required**. Items representing industry operator interpretation are labelled accordingly and are not legal authority.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing, "Rules on Ship Carbon Intensity and Rating System Enter into Force," IMO, 1 November 2022. https://www.imo.org/en/MediaCentre/PressBriefings/pages/CII-and-EEXI-entry-into-force.aspx
